title: melica teneriffae
intro: Melica teneriffae, also known as Canary Island melic, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family native to the Canary Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator effects. Herbal preparations of Melica teneriffae can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
